item.title: backlog check flags unmet dependsOn as a readiness gate
item.intent: backlog check must surface unmet dependencies so users know an item cannot safely run — dependency violations must be visible before sprint time, not discovered after.
item.description: backlog check <ID> currently validates title, intent, steps, and AC. It does not check whether items listed in dependsOn exist and are done. If an item depends on FEAT-001 and FEAT-001 is still todo, the check should warn (or fail, depending on mode). This is a companion to FEAT-001 (picker enforcement) — between the two, the system prevents dependency violations at both check-time and pick-time. Implementation: in the readiness gate logic, for each ID in dependsOn, verify the item exists in any backlog file and has status=done.
steps: In .aishore/aishore, find cmd_backlog_check() where readiness warnings are assembled
After existing checks (title, intent, steps, AC), add a loop over .dependsOn // []
For each dependency ID, call find_item (or scan backlog JSON files) to locate the item
If the dependency is not found, emit a warning: dependency ID not found in any backlog
If found but status != done, emit a warning showing the dependency ID and its current status
Ensure warning format matches other readiness gate messages
acceptanceCriteria: backlog check <ID> warns when any dependsOn item does not have status: done
Warning includes the dependency ID and its current status (or 'not found' if missing from all backlogs)
Items with no dependsOn field or an empty array pass this check without warnings
